prompt.spokenText: Look! One little bug is crawling on a leaf. Count it, then tap the number. How many bugs?
Two fluffy chicks are pecking for seeds. Count them one by one! How many chicks?
Tap the number two!
Which picture shows three frogs? Count each group and tap it!
Three slow turtles are walking to the pond. Count the turtles! How many are there?
Count the ladybugs in each group, then tap the group and its matching number!
Which picture shows two kittens? Tap it!
Tap the number three!
Four bunnies are hopping in the garden. Count every bunny! How many do you see?
Which picture shows four bees? Count each group carefully!
Five ducks are splashing in the pond. Count them all! How many ducks?
Tap the number four!
Count each group of little critters, then tap the group and its number!
Four pretty butterflies are flying in the sky. Count the butterflies!
Tap the number five!
Which picture shows five crabs? Count each group and tap it!
Wow, look at all the octopuses in the sea! Count every single one. How many octopuses?
The numbers got all mixed up! Put them in order, starting at one and counting up to five.
Which picture shows four dolphins? Count carefully — they look alike!
Tap the number five!
Four spiky hedgehogs are hiding in the leaves. Count the hedgehogs!
Count the owls in each group, then tap the group and its matching number!
Which group has five animals? Count each group carefully and tap it!
Tap the number four!
Six ladybugs landed on a big flower. Count them one by one! How many ladybugs?
Seven little birds are singing in the tree. Count the birds! How many are there?
Tap the number six!
Which picture shows six strawberries? Count each group and tap it!
Seven slow snails are sliding on the path. Count every snail!
Count the chicks in each group, then tap the group and its matching number!
Which picture shows seven balloons? Count carefully before they float away!
Tap the number seven!
Eight flowers bloomed in the garden today! Count every flower. How many are there?
Which picture shows eight penguins? Count each group of waddling penguins!
Nine cars are zooming down the road. Beep beep! Count the cars!
Tap the number eight!
Eight sleepy sheep are resting in the meadow. Count the sheep — but don't fall asleep!
Which picture shows nine stars? Count each twinkly group!
Count the grapes in each bunch, then tap the bunch and its matching number!
Tap the number nine!
Ten yummy cookies are on the plate! Count every cookie before someone eats them!
These numbers got mixed up! Put them in order, from six all the way up to ten.
Which picture shows ten ants marching? Count each line of ants!
Tap the number ten!
Nine crabs are scuttling on the sand. Count the crabs — click, click, click!
Count each group of animals, then tap the group and its matching number!
Which group has ten animals? Count each group carefully and tap it!
So many wiggly bugs! Take your time and count all the way to the end. How many bugs?
A circle is round like a ball, with no corners at all. Which one is the circle? Tap it!
Tap the square!
A square has four sides that are all the same size. Which one is the square? Tap it!
Which one is round like a circle? Look closely and tap it!
One of these is a circle — round and smooth with no corners. Tap the circle!
Match each shape to the thing that looks like it! The ball is round like a circle, and the gift box is a square.
Which one looks like a square, with four sides the same size? Tap it!
Tap the square!
A triangle has three sides and three pointy corners. Which one is the triangle? Tap it!
Tap the triangle!
A slice of pizza looks like a triangle! Which one is shaped like a triangle? Tap it!
Match each shape to its look-alike! The cookie is round, the sail is a triangle, and the dice is a square.
Do you remember the square, with its four equal sides? Tap the square!
Tap the circle!
Which one has three sides, just like a triangle? Tap it!
Picture a triangle with its pointy corners. How many corners does a triangle have? Tap the number!
A rectangle is like a stretched-out square — two long sides and two short sides. A door is a rectangle! Tap it!
Tap the one shaped like a rectangle!
Which one is shaped like a rectangle, with two long sides and two short sides? Tap it!
Two things in each pair have the same shape! Match the round ones, the triangle ones, and the rectangle ones.
Time for a shape check! Which one is a circle — perfectly round with no corners? Tap it!
Tap the square! Careful — one of these is a rectangle, not a square.
Which one looks like a square, with all four sides the same? Look very carefully!
Which shape has four sides that are all exactly the same size? Tap it!
So many butterflies! Count them one by one — go past ten! How many butterflies?
Tap the number twelve!
The apple tree dropped a big pile of apples! Count every apple in the orchard.
Which group has eleven chicks? Count by fives to go faster — five, ten, and then count on!
Count the stars in each group, then tap the group and its matching number!
Look at all the seashells on the beach! Count every shell. How many did you find?
Tap the number fourteen!
Which group has twelve daisies? Count each group carefully!
Buzz buzz! The hive is busy today. Count every single bee. How many bees?
Tap the number fifteen!
Which group has sixteen strawberries? Count by fives — five, ten, fifteen — then count on!
Wow, a big traffic jam! Count all the cars, one by one. How many cars are stuck?
Count the balloons in each bunch, then tap the bunch and its matching number!
Tap the number seventeen!
A whole pod of dolphins is jumping in the waves! Count every dolphin.
Which group has sixteen ladybugs? Count carefully — the groups are very close!
A big waddle of penguins is marching on the ice! Count them all. How many penguins?
Tap the number nineteen!
The pumpkin patch is full! Count every pumpkin — all the way up to the biggest number yet!
Which group has eighteen fish? Count by fives — five, ten, fifteen — then keep going!
Count the sunflowers and the mushrooms, then tap each group and its matching number!
Tap the number twenty!
The ducks are swimming in a long, long row. Count every duck! How many are there?
Which group has exactly twenty frogs? Count by fives if you can — five, ten, fifteen, twenty!
Two groups of elephants! Which group has more elephants? Tap it!
A monkey wants the bunch with more bananas! Which bunch has more? Tap it!
Tap the group that has more strawberries!
Woof woof! Which group has more puppies? Count both groups, then tap!
Match the groups that have the same amount! Count the red cars and the yellow taxis.
Balloons for the party! Which bunch has more balloons? Tap it!
Tap the group that has more ants!
Three groups this time! Which group has the most chicks? Count each one!
Fewer means not as many. Which group has fewer turtles? Tap it!
Which basket has fewer apples? Count both baskets, then tap the one with fewer!
Tap the group that has fewer bunnies!
Zoom! Which group has fewer airplanes flying in the sky? Tap it!
Match the treat groups that have the same amount! Count the donuts and the cupcakes.
Careful — this time find the group with MORE! Which group has more sailboats?
Tap the group that has fewer stars!
Three groups of parrots! Which group has the fewest? Count each group, then tap it!
Hold up one hand — that's five fingers! Tap the group that has the same amount as your five fingers.
Tricky one! These groups are very close. Count carefully — which group has more watermelons?
Listen to the drums: boom, boom, boom — that was three! Tap the group with the same amount, three drums.
Match each cherry group to the lemon group with the same amount!
The camels are lining up for a drink. Which group has fewer camels? Count carefully!
Tap the picture where the dogs and cats are equal — the same amount of each!
Super tricky! Count both groups of penguins all the way. Which group has more?
Which picture shows the same amount of suns and clouds — an equal amount of each?
The number parade is starting! Line up the numbers from smallest to biggest.
Count with me: one, two, three... what number comes next? Tap it!
These numbers are all mixed up! Put them in order from smallest to biggest.
Listen: seven, eight, nine... Tap the number that comes next!
What number comes just before five when you count? Tap it!
A number is missing from the parade! Four, something, six. Tap the missing number!
Match each number to the number that comes right after it!
Tap the number that comes right after four!
March the numbers into the parade, from smallest to biggest!
You counted all the way to ten! What number comes next? Tap it!
Listen: eleven, twelve, thirteen... Tap the number that comes next!
Five teen numbers got jumbled! Put them in order from smallest to biggest.
What number comes just before fifteen when you count? Tap it!
Match each number to the one that comes right after it!
Count on with me: twelve, thirteen, fourteen... what comes next? Tap it!
Tap the number that comes between thirteen and fifteen!
The biggest numbers are joining the parade! Line them up from smallest to biggest.
What number comes right after seventeen? Tap it!
Listen: eighteen, nineteen... Tap the number that comes next!
What number comes just before twenty when you count? Tap it!
These numbers skip around, but you can still line them up! Put them in order from smallest to biggest.
Match each number to the one that comes right after it!
Tap the number that comes between sixteen and eighteen!
Count to the very top: seventeen, eighteen, nineteen... what comes next? Tap it!
Count the ducks! How many do you see?
Count the stars! How many are there?
Two frogs are on a log. One more frog hops on! How many frogs now?
You have three apples. A friend gives you two more! How many apples do you have?
Some fish are swimming together. Count them all! How many fish?
One plus four equals what?
Which picture shows two plus two bunnies?
Four plus three equals what?
Five plus two equals what?
Count all the cookies on the plate! How many are there?
Six plus three equals what?
Which one makes ten?
Three bees are on a flower. Three more bees buzz over! How many bees now?
Two plus seven equals what?
Count the balloons before they float away! How many balloons?
Four plus six equals what?
Welcome to the picnic! Count the strawberries on the blanket. How many are there?
Six apples are on the blanket. Two ants carry two apples away! How many apples are left?
Three cookies are on the plate. A sneaky bird grabs one! How many cookies are left?
Four cupcakes are on a plate. Someone eats one! Tap how many cupcakes are left.
Five minus two equals what? Tap the number that finishes it.
Four minus two equals what?

prompt.text: Match each take-away to its answer.
prompt.spokenText: Match each take-away to its answer! Tap a problem, then tap the number that goes with it.
Six grapes are in the bowl. The ants march off with three! How many grapes are left?
Seven minus three equals what?
Eight birds sit on the picnic fence. Three fly away! How many birds are still sitting there?
Seven minus something equals four. How many were taken away? Tap the missing number.
Eight ants march to the picnic. Two turn around and go home! Tap how many ants keep marching.
Yum, watermelon! Count the slices before they get eaten. How many slices?
Ten minus two equals what?
The balloons pop one at a time! Tap the numbers counting back, from ten down to seven.
Nine minus five equals what? Tap the number that finishes it.
Ten sandwiches are in the basket. The ant team carries off six! How many sandwiches are left?
Some number minus three equals five. What number did we start with? Tap it.
Nine minus four equals what?
Ten berries are in the basket. Hungry birds eat seven! Tap how many berries are left.

prompt.text: Match each take-away to its answer.
prompt.spokenText: Match time! Tap each take-away problem, then tap its answer.
Which take-away makes two? Try each one!
Ten minus something equals four. How many were taken away? Tap the missing number.
Seven fireflies glow at the picnic. All seven fly away into the night! How many fireflies are left?
Which picture shows five crabs in all? Count both groups together!
Two plus something makes five. Tap the missing number!
Four plus what makes five?
Three ducks are swimming in the pond. How many more ducks would make five? Tap that number!
Match the number friends! Tap two numbers that add up to five.
Count the flowers by the bridge! How many do you see?
Which two numbers add up to five?
Something plus one makes five. Tap the missing number!
Six plus what makes ten?
Seven plus something makes ten. Tap the missing number!
Cross the bridge! Tap two numbers that add up to ten.
Two ladybugs sit on a leaf. How many more ladybugs would make ten? Tap that number!
Which two numbers add up to ten?
Count the seashells under the bridge! How many are there?
Ten is the same as three plus something. Tap the missing number!
One plus what makes ten?
Three plus seven makes ten. So ten minus seven equals what?
Something plus six makes ten. Tap the missing number!

prompt.text: Match each sum to its total.
prompt.spokenText: Match each adding problem to its total! Tap a problem, then tap its answer.
You have ten crayons. Two are red and the rest are blue. Tap how many crayons are blue!
Two plus eight equals ten! Which take-away fact is in the same family?
Cross the number bridge! Tap the numbers from smallest to biggest.
Ten minus something equals five. Tap the missing number!
Zara needs ten stickers to finish her page. She has eight. How many more stickers does she need?
Nine plus four! Give one to the nine to make ten, then add the three left over. What do you get?
Nine plus four is the same as ten plus something. Tap the missing number!
Eight mountain goats stand on the cliff. Four more climb up to join them! How many goats in all?
Ten plus five! Tap the sum.
Eight plus three equals what? Hop to ten first, then keep going!

prompt.text: Match each ten-plus to its sum.
prompt.spokenText: Ten plus a little more! Match each problem to its sum.
Nine plus five equals what? Make a ten to help you!
Eight plus four is the same as ten plus something. Tap the missing number!
Fifteen minus six! Take away five to land on ten, then take away one more. What is left?
The hikers packed fourteen trail cookies. They munch six on the way up! How many cookies are left?
Thirteen minus three equals what? Tap the answer!
Seven plus eight! Tap the sum.
So many tents at base camp! Count every tent. How many are there?
Eleven minus five equals what?
Climb the mountain! Tap the trail markers from the smallest number to the biggest.
Nine plus something equals sixteen. Tap the missing number!
Eight plus seven equals what? Make a ten to help you climb!
Fourteen minus something equals eight. Tap the missing number!
Eighteen stars twinkle over the mountain. Clouds drift in and hide nine of them! How many stars still twinkle?
Sixteen minus nine! Tap the answer.

prompt.text: Match each problem to its answer.
prompt.spokenText: Summit match! Tap each problem, then tap its answer.
Which adding problem makes twelve?
Something plus six equals fifteen. Tap the missing number!
Twenty flags fly at the summit. A gust of wind blows eight away! How many flags are still flying?
Four tens and two ones! What number does that build?
Two tens and zero ones. What number is that? Tap it!

prompt.text: Which number is 3 tens and 5 ones?
prompt.spokenText: Which number is three tens and five ones?
Tap the number that has six tens and one one!

prompt.text: Match each number to its tens and ones.
prompt.spokenText: Match each number to its blocks! Tap a number, then tap its tens and ones.
Seventy! How many tens are inside it?
One tower floor holds exactly ten bricks. Count these bricks. How many are there?
Five tens and nine ones! What number does that build?
Stack the tower! Tap the floors counting by tens, starting at ten.
Which number is bigger, forty-seven or fifty-one? Check the tens first!
Eight tens and three ones. What number is that? Tap it!
Tap the number fifty-four!
Thirty-seven! How many ones does it have?

prompt.text: Match each number to its blocks.
prompt.spokenText: Match each number to its tens and ones! Tap a number, then tap its match.
Which number is smaller, sixty-eight or eighty-six?
Seventy-two is some tens and two ones. How many tens? Tap the number!
Thirty-nine has three tens. Sixty-one has six tens. Tap the number with more tens!
Six tens and six ones. What number is that? Tap it!
Add one more floor to the tower! What number is ten more than forty-five?
Tap the number that is ten less than seventy-three!
Careful, the digits are tricky! Tap the numbers from smallest to biggest.
Which blocks build ninety?
Fifty-eight is five tens and some ones. How many ones? Tap the number!
Almost at the top! What number is ten more than eighty-nine?
Which picture shows two mittens plus two mittens?
Double four! Four plus four equals what?
Double five! Five plus five. Tap the answer!
Socks come in twos! Count every sock. How many are there?
Double three! Three plus three equals what? Tap the answer!

prompt.text: Match each double to its answer.
prompt.spokenText: Double match! Tap each double, then tap its answer.
Twin puppies each dig up three bones. How many bones did they dig up in all?
Which double makes eight?
Double six! Six plus six equals what?
Double eight! Eight plus eight equals what?
Double seven! Seven plus seven equals what? Tap the answer!
Double nine! Nine plus nine. Tap the answer!

prompt.text: Match each double to its answer.
prompt.spokenText: Double match! Tap each double, then tap its answer.
Which double makes eighteen?
The doubles staircase! Tap the answers from smallest to biggest.
Something plus eight equals sixteen. It is a double! Tap the missing number.
Six plus seven! Think: double six is twelve, then one more. What is it?
Six plus seven is the same as six plus six plus one more. Tap the missing number!
Five plus six! Think: double five is ten, then one more. What is it?
Eight plus nine! Think: double eight is sixteen, then one more. Tap the answer!
Seven plus eight! Double seven, then one more. What is it?

prompt.text: Match each near double to its answer.
prompt.spokenText: Near-double match! Tap each problem, then tap its answer.
Nine plus eight! Double nine is eighteen, so take one away. Tap the answer!
Which near double makes eleven?
All aboard the Skip-Count Express! Let's count by twos. Two, four, six... what comes next?
Three pairs of socks are hanging on the line. Count them by twos! How many socks in all?
Listen closely! When you count by twos, tap the number that comes right after six.
These train cars got mixed up! Put the numbers in order, counting by twos from smallest to biggest.
Keep counting by twos! Four, six, eight... which number fills the blank?
The train is picking up speed! Ten, twelve, fourteen... what number comes next when counting by twos?

prompt.text: Match each number to the next!
prompt.spokenText: Match each number to the number that comes next when you count by twos!
One of these lists counts by twos. Which one is it?
Now let's count by fives! Five, ten, fifteen... what comes next?
The train jumps by tens! Ten, twenty, thirty... which number fills the blank?
Listen up! Tap the number that comes after thirty-five when counting by fives.
Line up the train cars by tens, from smallest to biggest!
Three hands are waving hello! Each hand has five fingers. Count by fives... how many fingers in all?

prompt.text: Match each number to the next!
prompt.spokenText: Match each number to the number that comes next when you count by fives!
Listen carefully! When you count by tens, tap the number that comes right after sixty.
One of these lists counts by tens. Which one is it?
The express starts in the middle this time! Twelve, fourteen, sixteen... what comes next when counting by twos?
Count by fives from twenty-five! Twenty-five, thirty, thirty-five... which number fills the blank?
These stations got scrambled! Put them in order, counting by fives from smallest to biggest.
Start at forty and count by tens. Forty, fifty, sixty, seventy... tap the number that comes right after seventy!
A number fell off the train! Thirty, forty, something, sixty. Which number is missing?

prompt.text: Match each pattern to its rule!
prompt.spokenText: Match each counting pattern to its rule. Is it counting by twos, by fives, or by tens?
Now the train rolls backwards by tens! Ninety, eighty, seventy... what comes next?
Last stop! One of these lists counts by fives. Which one is it?
We're hiking up the hundred chart! What number is one more than forty-six?
Take one step back! What number is one less than thirty?
Keep walking along the path! Fifty-eight, fifty-nine... which number comes next?
Listen closely! Tap the number that is one more than seventy-nine.
Big hop! On the hundred chart, hopping down one row adds ten. What is ten more than twenty-three?
The trail markers fell over! Put them back in counting order, from smallest to biggest.
Match each number to the number that is ten more!
Hop back up a row! What number is ten less than sixty?
Time for ten-jumps! What number is ten more than forty-two?
Keep jumping by tens! Thirty-four, forty-four, fifty-four... which number fills the blank?
Listen up, hiker! Tap the number that is ten less than sixty-five.
Step back one row! What number is ten less than eighty?
Let's add tens! Forty plus thirty equals what? Think: four tens plus three tens.
Put these numbers in order from smallest to biggest. Each one is a ten-jump apart!
Two hills the same size! Thirty plus thirty equals what?
Match each tens problem to its answer!
We're near the top of the chart! What number is ten more than eighty-seven?
Take away tens! Seventy minus twenty equals what? Think: seven tens take away two tens.
Sixty minus forty equals what? Count back four tens from sixty!
Here's a tricky one! Tap the number that is one less than one hundred.
A trail marker is missing! Forty-five, fifty-five, something, seventy-five. Each jump adds ten. Which number is missing?
Order the checkpoint numbers from smallest to biggest. Each one is ten apart!
Match each ten-jump problem to its answer!
Last big jump! Ninety minus thirty equals what?
Tens and ones make a great team! Thirty plus four equals what?
Two tens and six ones team up! What number do they make?
Forty-two plus five equals what? The five joins the ones!
Twenty-one plus seven equals what? Add the seven to the ones!
Listen closely! Forty plus six. Tap the answer!
Match each team of tens and ones to the number it makes!
Thirty-four plus twenty equals what? The twenty joins the tens!
Watch those tens and ones! Put these numbers in order from smallest to biggest.
Two teams join up! Thirty-two plus fifteen equals what? Add the tens, then the ones!
Twenty-three plus thirty-four equals what? Tens with tens, ones with ones!
Forty-one plus twenty-six equals what? Fill in the answer!
Listen up! Fifty-two plus thirteen. Tap the sum!
Twenty-four kids are riding the bus. At the next stop, twelve more climb on! How many kids are on the bus now?
Match each addition problem to its answer!
Thirty-six plus forty-two equals what? You've got this!
One more with your ears! Forty-four plus twenty-two. Tap the answer!
Now the team takes away! Forty-seven minus twenty-three equals what? Subtract the tens, then the ones!
Fifty-eight minus six equals what? Take the six from the ones!
Sixty-eight minus twenty-five equals what? Fill in the answer!
Listen carefully! Seventy-six minus forty-one. Tap the difference!
You have thirty-nine shiny stickers. You give seventeen to your best friend! How many stickers do you have left?
Match each subtraction problem to its answer!
Surprise, it's a plus! Sixty-five plus twenty-three equals what?
These numbers look alike, but watch the tens and ones! Put them in order from smallest to biggest.
Two baskets each hold three apples. Count them all together! How many apples do the group gatherers have?
Which picture shows three groups with two kittens in each group?
The garden has two rows of three sunflowers. Count every sunflower! How many are there?
Two groups of four stars are twinkling. Count them all! How many stars in the sky?
Tap the picture that shows two groups of five fish!

prompt.text: Match each group to its number!
prompt.spokenText: Match each bunch of grapes to the number that tells how many there are in all!
Two groups of three! Count them up and fill in the blank. How many in all?
Four groups of two turtles are crawling along. Count them all! How many turtles?
Three groups of two fish swim by. Two plus two plus two! How many fish in all?
Three groups of three! Three plus three plus three. Fill in the blank with the total.
Two boxes each hold five donuts. Five plus five! How many donuts altogether?
Two groups of four ducks are paddling. Four plus four! How many ducks in the pond?
Two plus two plus two plus two. Four groups of two! Tap the answer.

prompt.text: Match the adding to its total!
prompt.spokenText: Match each repeated addition to the number that tells its total!
Five groups of two! Add all the twos together and fill in the blank.
Two groups of three ladybugs are resting on a leaf. Three plus three! How many ladybugs?
Three groups of two strawberries is the same as three times two. What does three times two equal?
Two times four means two groups of four. What does two times four equal?
Five times two means five groups of two. Fill in the blank with the answer!
Two times five! That is two groups of five. Tap the answer.
Four times two means four groups of two. Which picture of baby chicks shows four times two?

prompt.text: Match each times fact to its total!
prompt.spokenText: Match each times fact to the number that tells how much it equals!
Three times three means three groups of three. What does three times three equal?
Two times five means two groups of five. What does two times five equal?
Look at the clock! The little hand points to the three and the big hand points to the twelve. What time is it?
Tap the clock that shows six o'clock!
The little hand points to the nine and the big hand points to the twelve. What time does the clock show?
Which clock shows twelve o'clock, when both hands point straight up to the twelve?
The big hand points to the twelve and the little hand points to the eight. What time is it?

prompt.text: Match each clock to its time!
prompt.spokenText: Match each clock to the time that it shows!
The little hand points to the five and the big hand points to the twelve. It is five o'clock! Fill in the blank.
The little hand points to the two and the big hand points to the twelve. What time does the clock show?
The big hand points down to the six, so it is half past! The little hand is just past the two. It is half past two. What time is it?
Tap the clock that shows half past four!
The big hand points down to the six for half past, and the little hand is just past the twelve. It is half past twelve. What time is it?
Half past six is the same as six thirty. Fill in the blank with the right time!
Which clock shows half past nine, with the little hand just past the nine?

prompt.text: Match each clock to its time!
prompt.spokenText: Match each clock to the half past time that it shows!
Tap the clock that shows half past eight!
The big hand points down to the six for half past, and the little hand is just past the three. What time is it?
It is five o'clock now. What time will it be one hour later?
The big hand points down to the six for half past, and the little hand is just past the six. What time is it?

prompt.text: One hour after 3 o'clock is ___ o'clock
prompt.spokenText: It is three o'clock. One hour later the little hand moves to the next number. Fill in the blank!

payload.template: One hour after 3 o'clock is ___ o'clock

prompt.spokenText: It is eight o'clock now. Tap the clock that shows one hour later!
The big hand points down to the six for half past, and the little hand is just past the ten. What time is it?

prompt.text: Match each clock to its time!
prompt.spokenText: Match each clock to the time that it shows. Watch out, some are o'clock and some are half past!
It is half past two. One hour later it will be half past the next number. What time is that?
It is twelve o'clock now. What time will it be one hour later?

prompt.text: A nickel 🪙 is worth how much?
prompt.spokenText: Here is a shiny nickel! How many cents is a nickel worth?
Tap the coin that is worth ten cents!

prompt.text: How much is a dime worth? 🪙
prompt.spokenText: Here is a little dime. How many cents is a dime worth?
A penny is the smallest coin. Which coin is worth just one cent?

prompt.text: Match each coin to its value!
prompt.spokenText: Match each coin to the number of cents it is worth!
A quarter is the biggest coin here. How many cents is a quarter worth? Fill in the blank!

prompt.text: How much is a quarter worth? 🪙
prompt.spokenText: Here is a big quarter. How many cents is a quarter worth?

prompt.text: Which coin is worth the most?
prompt.spokenText: A penny, a nickel, and a dime are on the table. Which coin is worth the most cents?
Count these three pennies. Each penny is worth one cent. How many cents in all?
Here are two nickels. Each nickel is worth five cents. Count by fives! How much is that?
Two dimes! Each dime is worth ten cents. Count by tens and fill in the blank.
You have one dime worth ten cents and one nickel worth five cents. How much altogether?
You have one quarter and one penny. Tap how much you have in all!
Five pennies make five cents, and one nickel makes five cents too. How much together?

prompt.text: Match each coin group to its total!
prompt.spokenText: Match each little pile of coins to the number of cents it is worth in all!
One dime is ten cents, plus two pennies. How many cents in all?
You want to make exactly thirty cents. Which coins should the collector pick?
You want to make exactly fifteen cents. Which coins make that amount?
Two dimes make twenty cents, and one nickel makes five more. Fill in the blank with the total!
Tap the coins that make exactly twenty cents!
You want to make exactly fifty cents. Which coins make that amount?

prompt.text: Match each amount to its coins!
prompt.spokenText: Match each amount of cents to the coins that make it!
You want to make exactly six cents. Which coins make that amount?
You want to make exactly thirty-five cents. Which coins make that amount?

prompt.text: 2 baskets of 3 apples. How many apples?
prompt.spokenText: Let's start the trek! Two baskets each hold three apples. That's two groups of three. How many apples in all?

prompt.text: 2 rows of 4 stars. Count them all!
prompt.spokenText: Two rows of stars are twinkling. Each row has four stars. That's two times four. Count all the stars!
Two times five. Two groups of five! What is the answer?
Listen closely! Two times two. Tap the number that shows two groups of two.
The trail jumps by tens! Three times ten means three groups of ten. What is three times ten?
Two times something equals six. Which number fills the blank?

prompt.text: Match each fact to its answer!
prompt.spokenText: Match each times-two fact to its answer. Two times the number gives the product!
Last stop on the trailhead! Four times ten. Four groups of ten. What is the answer?

prompt.text: 3 hands of 5 fingers. How many fingers?
prompt.spokenText: Three hands are waving hello! Each hand has five fingers. That's three times five. How many fingers in all?

prompt.text: 3 rows of 3 flowers. Count them all!
prompt.spokenText: Three rows of flowers are blooming. Each row has three flowers. That's three times three. Count all the flowers!
Five times four. Five groups of four! What is the answer?
Listen up! Five times two. Tap the number that shows five groups of two.
Three times six. Three groups of six! What is the answer?
Five times something equals twenty-five. Which number fills the blank?

prompt.text: Match each fact to its answer!
prompt.spokenText: Match each times-three fact to its answer!
Five times five. Five groups of five! What is the answer?
We're near the top! Six times three. What is the answer?
Four times five. Four groups of five! What is the answer?
Something times four equals twenty. Which number fills the blank?
Listen carefully! Seven times five. Tap the number that shows seven groups of five.
Eight times five. Eight groups of five! What is the answer?

prompt.text: Order by their answers, smallest first!
prompt.spokenText: Each card is a times fact. Work out each answer, then put the cards in order from the smallest answer to the biggest!
Nine times five. Nine groups of five! What is the answer?
Summit reached! One last fact. Six times four. What is the answer?

prompt.text: 12 cookies shared by 3 friends. How many each?
prompt.spokenText: The Sharing Squad has twelve cookies. Three friends share them fairly. How many cookies does each friend get?

prompt.text: 6 strawberries on 2 plates. How many on each?
prompt.spokenText: Six strawberries are shared onto two plates, the same on each. How many strawberries are on one plate?

prompt.text: 10 apples shared by 5 kids. How many each?
prompt.spokenText: Ten apples are shared fairly by five kids. How many apples does each kid get?
Listen closely! Eight balloons are shared fairly by two friends. Tap how many balloons each friend gets.

prompt.text: 9 acorns onto 3 branches. Count one branch!
prompt.spokenText: Nine acorns are shared fairly onto three branches. Count how many acorns are on just one branch!

prompt.text: 8 pizzas shared by 4 friends. How many each?
prompt.spokenText: Eight pizzas are shared fairly by four friends. How many pizzas does each friend get?

prompt.text: Match each sharing story to its answer!
prompt.spokenText: Match each fair-sharing story to how many each friend gets!

prompt.text: Which plate shows a fair share?
prompt.spokenText: Two friends should get the very same amount. Which picture shows a fair share, with the same number of candies in each group?
Here comes the divide sign! Twelve divided by three means twelve shared into three equal groups. What is the answer?
Ten divided by two. Ten shared into two equal groups. What is the answer?
Fifteen divided by something equals five. Which number fills the blank?
Listen up! Twelve divided by two. Tap the answer.
Fifteen oranges shared into five equal bowls. That's fifteen divided by five. How many oranges in each bowl?
Twenty divided by four. Twenty shared into four equal groups. What is the answer?

prompt.text: Match each divide fact to its answer!
prompt.spokenText: Match each divide fact to its answer!
Eighteen divided by three. Eighteen shared into three equal groups. What is the answer?
Multiplication and division are a team! Three times four is twelve. So what is twelve divided by four?
Twenty divided by four. Which number fills the blank?
A fact family uses the same three numbers. Which fact belongs with two, five, and ten?
Listen carefully! Two times six is twelve. Tap the answer to twelve divided by six.

prompt.text: Match each times fact to its divide partner!
prompt.spokenText: Every times fact has a divide partner in the same family. Match each one to its partner!
Twenty-four divided by four. Twenty-four shared into four equal groups. What is the answer?
Five times five is twenty-five. So what is twenty-five divided by five?
The last family uses three, six, and eighteen. Which fact completes it?
Welcome to the Fraction Picnic! There are two apples. One is red and one is green. What fraction of the apples are red?

prompt.text: 🍕 cut into 2 equal slices. You eat 1. What fraction?
prompt.spokenText: A pizza is cut into two equal slices. You eat one slice. What fraction of the pizza did you eat?

prompt.text: 🍓🍓🍓🍓 — eat half. How many is that?
prompt.spokenText: There are four strawberries. You eat half of them. How many strawberries is that?
Listen closely! Tap the fraction that means one half.

prompt.text: 🐟🐟🐟🐟 — half swim away. How many swim away?
prompt.spokenText: Four fish are in the pond. Half of them swim away. How many fish swim away?

prompt.text: Match each group to half of it!
prompt.spokenText: Match each group of snacks to half of it!
Half of eight is what number? Which chip fills the blank?

prompt.text: 🍪🍪🍪🍪🍪🍪 shared by 2 friends. What fraction does each get?
prompt.spokenText: Six cookies are shared equally between two friends. What fraction of the cookies does each friend get?

prompt.text: 🍕 cut into 4 equal slices. You eat 1. What fraction?
prompt.spokenText: A pizza is cut into four equal slices. You eat one slice. What fraction of the pizza did you eat?
There are four toy cars. One of them is red. What fraction of the cars are red?

prompt.text: 🍌🍌🍌🍌🍌🍌🍌🍌 — eat a quarter. How many is that?
prompt.spokenText: There are eight bananas. You eat a quarter of them. How many bananas is that?
Listen up! Tap the fraction that means one quarter.
A half and a quarter are both pieces of the same treat. Which piece is bigger, one half or one quarter?

prompt.text: Match each group to a quarter of it!
prompt.spokenText: Match each group of picnic snacks to a quarter of it!
A quarter of eight is what number? Which chip fills the blank?

prompt.text: 🍕 has 4 slices. 3 are eaten. What fraction is eaten?
prompt.spokenText: A pizza has four equal slices. Three of them are eaten. What fraction of the pizza has been eaten?
There are three oranges. One of them is peeled. What fraction of the oranges are peeled?

prompt.text: 🍫 broken into 3 equal pieces. You eat 1. What fraction?
prompt.spokenText: A chocolate bar breaks into three equal pieces. You eat one piece. What fraction of the bar did you eat?

prompt.text: 🌭🌭🌭🌭🌭🌭🌭🌭🌭 — eat a third. How many is that?
prompt.spokenText: There are nine hot dogs. You eat a third of them. How many hot dogs is that?
Listen carefully! Tap the fraction that means one third.
Which piece of the picnic cake is bigger, one third or one half?
Here are pieces of the same picnic pie. Put the fractions in order from the smallest piece to the biggest, ending with the whole pie!
A third of nine is what number? Which chip fills the blank?

prompt.text: 🍉🍉🍉 — 2 are ripe. What fraction is ripe?
prompt.spokenText: There are three watermelons. Two of them are ripe. What fraction of the watermelons are ripe?
Blast off with big numbers! Three hundreds, four tens, and two ones make what number?
Look at the number seven hundred fifty-eight. The seven sits in the hundreds spot. How much is it worth?
Add the parts together! Two hundred plus fifty plus six equals what number?
This number has five hundreds, zero tens, and nine ones. What number is it?
Use your ears! Six hundreds, zero tens, and three ones. Tap that number!
Four hundreds, one ten, and eight ones team up. What big number do they make?
Match each set of hundreds, tens, and ones to the number it makes!
These numbers use the same digits, so check the hundreds first! Put them in order from smallest to biggest.
The ones make more than ten, so we carry! Twenty-seven plus fifteen equals what?
Forty-eight plus thirty-six equals what? Eight and six make fourteen, so carry a ten!
Fifty-six plus twenty-eight equals what? Six plus eight is fourteen — carry the ten!
Thirty-seven plus twenty-five equals what? Add the ones, carry, then the tens!
Listen closely! Nineteen plus seven. Tap the sum!
Ears ready! Forty-five plus twenty-nine. Tap the answer!
Every one of these needs a carry! Match each problem to its sum.
Fifty-eight rockets wait on the launch pad. Twenty-four more roll out! How many rockets in all?
Countdown time! Fifty-two minus twenty-seven equals what? You may need to borrow a ten.
Eighty-four minus thirty-nine equals what? Borrow a ten so you can take away the nine!
Now a three-digit blast! Three hundred fifty-six minus one hundred twenty-four equals what?
Four hundred seventy-five minus one hundred thirty equals what? Subtract the tens and hundreds!
Use your ears for this one! Ninety-one minus forty-six. Tap the difference!
A space station has six hundred forty-two stars painted on it. Two hundred eighteen fade away! How many stars are left?
Match each take-away problem to its answer!
These numbers all use fours, fives, and zeros! Check the hundreds and tens, then order them from smallest to biggest.
Times ten just adds a zero! Three times ten equals what?
Thirty times two equals what? Think three tens, then double it!
Ten times seven equals what? Just pop a zero onto the seven!
Five times ten equals what? Fill in the answer!
Use your ears! Ten times four. Tap the answer!
Forty times two equals what? Four tens, doubled!
Match each times-ten problem to its answer!
Each of these is a number times ten! Put the answers in order from smallest to biggest.
Let's break fourteen times three into pieces! Start with the tens: ten times three equals what?
Now the ones part of fourteen times three! Four times three equals what?
Put the pieces back together! Thirty plus twelve equals what? That's fourteen times three!
Break apart thirteen times two! Ten times two is twenty, three times two is six. What's the total?
Twelve times four breaks into forty plus something. Two times four fills the blank. What is it?
Break it apart in your head! Fifteen times two. Tap the answer!
Break each one apart, then match it to its answer!
Each side of the rocket has sixteen windows. Both sides means sixteen times two! How many windows in all?
Full thrust! Twenty-one times four equals what? Twenty times four, plus one times four.
Thirty-two times three equals what? Thirty times three is ninety, two times three is six!
Fifteen times six equals what? Ten sixes is sixty, five sixes is thirty!
Twenty-four times five equals what? Fill in the answer!
Ears ready! Thirteen times five. Tap the product!
Forty-three times four equals what? Forty times four is one hundred sixty, three times four is twelve!
Match each big multiply problem to its product!

prompt.text: Order the products, smallest to biggest!
prompt.spokenText: Here are four products from our mission! Put them in order from smallest to biggest.
One half can wear a different name! Which fraction is the same size as one half?

prompt.text: Which shows the same as one half?
prompt.spokenText: Which picture has the same amount shaded as one half — that means half the boxes are blue!
One half is the same as how many fourths? Fill in the top number!
Two fourths has a shorter name too! Which fraction is the same size as two fourths?
Use your ears! Which fraction is the same size as two fourths? Tap it!
Which picture has two of its four boxes shaded green? That's two fourths!
Match each fraction to another fraction that is exactly the same size!
Two fourths is really one out of how many equal pieces? Fill in the bottom number!
Same size pieces, but a different number of them! Which is bigger, three fourths or one fourth?
The bottoms match, so count the top! Which is smaller, four fifths or two fifths?
When the bottoms match, more pieces on top means more. Is three sixths less or more than five sixths?
All of these are eighths! The one with the most pieces wins. Which is the biggest?
Use your ears! Which is bigger, three sevenths or six sevenths? Tap the bigger one!
All of these are sixths! The one with the fewest pieces is smallest. Which one is it?
These are all fifths! Put them in order from smallest to biggest by counting the top numbers.
One more with your ears! Which is more, seven ninths or two ninths? Tap the bigger piece!
When fewer friends share, each piece is bigger! Which is bigger, one half or one third?
More pieces means each one is smaller! Which is smaller, one fourth or one sixth?
Cutting into three pieces or five pieces — which makes bigger pieces? Is one third bigger or smaller than one fifth?
One whole cut into halves, fourths, or eighths. The fewest cuts make the biggest piece! Which is the biggest?
Use your ears! Which is bigger, one third or one sixth? Tap the bigger piece!
Match each fraction to what it means — one piece out of that many equal pieces!
Careful — biggest to smallest this time! The fewer the pieces, the bigger each one. Order one half, one fourth, one eighth.

prompt.text: Bigger piece: shared by 2 or by 3?
prompt.spokenText: A moon cake shared by two astronauts is one half each. Shared by three is one third each. Which piece is bigger?
